stem: A factory produces pharmaceutical tablets using two sources. Batch Process Alpha handles 66% of production with a known defect rate of 2.7%. Batch Process Beta handles 34% of production, but its defect rate is uncertain — recent quality audits suggest it lies between 5.2% and 8.0%, depending on raw material batch quality. A randomly selected pharmaceutical tablet is found to be defective. What is the probability it came from Batch Process Beta?

ground_truth.answer: P(Batch Process Beta | defective) ranges from 0.498 to 0.604 as Batch Process Beta's defect rate varies from 5.2% to 8.0%. Point estimate at 6.6%: 0.557.

solution_steps: Apply Bayes' theorem: P(B|def) = P(def|B)×P(B) / [P(def|A)×P(A) + P(def|B)×P(B)].
The interval [0.498, 0.604] captures the range of posterior probabilities.
common_failure_modes: Using only the midpoint defect rate and providing a single answer
Comparing defect rates directly without applying Bayes' theorem
